summary: Validated NTFS memory-usage control. Microsoft documents NtfsMemoryUsage as a two-state fsutil behavior surface: 1 is the default and 2 raises NTFS memory thresholds. The app now writes the documented default value 1, so the record matches research.

setting.name: NTFS Memory Usage
setting.casual_explanation: NTFS has an internal memory-usage tuning that affects its cache behavior. Most people should leave it at the default unless they have a proven file-server style workload.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it only when they are tuning NTFS behavior on systems that open and close many files repeatedly.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The official Microsoft control is a small two-state setting. The app now writes the documented default, so the record is publishable as a validated default-reset control.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents fsutil behavior memoryusage as updating HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\NtfsMemoryUsage. The supported values are 1 and 2. Microsoft states that 1 is the default and 2 increases NTFS lookaside lists and memory thresholds. The app now writes NtfsMemoryUsage = 1 under the same path, matching the documented default.

allowed_values.label: Default NTFS memory usage
allowed_values.meaning: NTFS uses the default amount of paged-pool memory.

allowed_values.label: Raised NTFS memory usage
allowed_values.meaning: NTFS increases its lookaside lists and memory thresholds.

app_current_implementation.notes: The provider writes NtfsMemoryUsage = 1, which matches the documented default for this control.

writes.notes: Observed app value now matches the documented default.

windows_defaults.label: Windows default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the documented NTFS memory-usage baseline
windows_defaults.notes: This record does not publish the app's previous 0 value as a supported equivalent of the default.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Research comparisons against the current app write
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the official default NTFS memory behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are trying to document the current app implementation as-is.
tradeoffs: The current app's unsupported 0 value is not published.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: fsutil behavior
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents memoryusage as updating HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\NtfsMemoryUsage, with supported values 1 and 2 and a default of 1.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NtfsMemoryUsage = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents NtfsMemoryUsage as a two-state fsutil behavior control with 1 as the default and 2 as the raised-memory option. The provider now writes the documented default value 1, so the record matches research and can be published as a validated reset-to-default control.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Set to 1 or 2. When set to 1 (the default), NTFS uses the default amount of paged-pool memory.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft documents fsutil behavior memoryusage as updating NtfsMemoryUsage, with 1 as the default and 2 as the raised-memory option.
